opp.name: Bright Horizons 2025 - S2P
opp.next_step: Vendor selection call on Monday Final commerical pack sent today - Andrew back in office Wednesday - feedback end of week start of next Commercial Feedback with Andrew and Elaine by 18th June Decision pending in June Contract Redlining and Signing July POC was successful - best Platform
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is eProcurement, iSource, iSupplier and Merlin Intake ‚Äî Merlin Intake is the sole AI module in this S2P deal at the education-services account. No Agentic AI or ANA. The single-AI-SKU bundling within a 4-product S2P scope is a faint AI signal ‚Äî Merlin Intake is more of a bundled value-add than a deal headline.
• Engaged BD, Shortlisted stage with 11 valid Avoma meetings on file. Recent activity is heavy with 17 BH attendees at the 18 May 2026 scheduled meeting, 19 BH attendees at the 24 Apr 2026 session, and large 26 Mar 2026 (~115min completed) and 10 Jul 2025 (~119min completed) workshops with Andrew Duckworth and Elaine Rymill. Multi-hour multi-stakeholder workshops are heavy buyer-side investment.
• SF record fetch timed out this run; despite very strong multi-stakeholder engagement, AI product scope is limited to a single Merlin Intake module so the boundary defaulted UP at 4/5 to 5 (small upgrade from prior 4) reflecting Engaged + heavy meeting cadence. Medium confidence. Score 5 reflects the engagement depth without proportional AI product breadth.

sources: Avoma Drop In Session AP 9 Jun 2026
metrics.narrative: The pain is well evidenced but the value case is qualitative, not quantified by the buyer. In the POC the AP team saw a 97 percent automatic invoice match ('the success rate of all the invoices which you have sent was roughly 97 percent', Nishita, 9 Jun 2026) with line items extracted automatically, and nursery managers valued the basket and catalog efficiency. Zycus asserted up to 30 percent better spend management (Anthony Gray, 26 Mar 2026), but Bright Horizons has never put its own numbers (hours saved, headcount, savings target) on the business case. Quantifying ROI with finance (Callum Waring / Martin Grover) before EB sign-off is the move.

champion.narrative: There are two aligned advocates. Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) leads the tender and drives the commercials and vendor selection. Elaine Rymill (Procurement Manager) is the daily operational champion coordinating every session, the sandbox rollout, and the SOW and MSA logistics, in near-daily email contact through 1 to 2 Jul 2026. Their advocacy is proven: Bright Horizons chose to partner with Zycus on 30 Jun 2026. What is still missing is champion access to the economic buyer, neither has yet pulled Joanne Urso or the CFO into a direct Zycus conversation to lock the budget sign-off.

competition.narrative: Zycus has won the selection. Bright Horizons down-selected from a shortlist that included GEP and Coupa and chose Zycus after a successful POC (Next Step: 'POC was successful - best Platform'; selection confirmed 30 Jun 2026). Proactis is the incumbent P2P provider being displaced, contract expiring Mar 2027. The only live competitive threat now is Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal if the contracting timeline slips past the Nov 2026 kickoff window. The wedge is a phased P2P-first rollout aligned to BH's finance constraints, with Merlin Intake AI positioned as the Phase 2 value unlock.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is concrete and owner-attributed. Nursery managers struggle with the current clunky system: baskets 'never worked' and product search 'you really have to be specific' (Lauren Ross, 2 Jun 2026), and Lauren asked for order-cutoff-time visibility she does not have today. AP (Stuart Lamont, Bev Patrick) carries manual invoice and receipt effort that the POC three-way match largely removed. Procurement (Elaine Rymill, Andrew Duckworth) is replacing the outdated Proactis P2P stack with a full source-to-pay platform. Pain owners span nurseries (Lauren Ross), AP (Stuart Lamont, Bev Patrick) and procurement (Elaine Rymill).

paper_process.narrative: Paper is now the critical path. The revised MSA (amended to the UK entity for credit) and the Order Form were sent to Elaine Rymill on 2 Jul 2026, and a two-hour, two-part SOW scoping workshop is being scheduled (Zycus proposed dates 1 Jul; BH asked for prep time). Signature will run through Bright Horizons' own DocuSign license, and contracts flow back into Zycus. Legal owner Catherine Wilson and IT/InfoSec (Michael White, Workday integration) have not yet been engaged this cycle. The Proactis contract expiry in Mar 2027 and the required Nov 2026 kickoff are the forcing functions; getting legal and InfoSec started in parallel this week is what protects the 31 Jul date.

sources: SF internal note 16 Jun 2026 (Boston executive meetings)
economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority for the ~$251k investment sits above the working team and is mapped but not yet engaged. Joanne Urso (Vice President, Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) and the CFO were named for a Boston executive validation meeting in an internal note dated 16 Jun 2026, but neither has appeared on any recorded call and no written budget sign-off is on record. Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement) is driving the commercials but cannot unilaterally commit the spend. Converting the Boston executive discussion into written approval from Joanne Urso is the remaining power gate before signature.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal tender that has run its course: discovery, Stage 2 supplier demonstrations (24 Apr 2026), pricing and model session (13 May 2026), a two-week hands-on POC across sourcing, AP and nurseries (kickoff 18 May 2026), then vendor selection and selection of Zycus over the GEP and Coupa shortlist (confirmed 30 Jun 2026). The approval chain runs Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement) up to Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and the CFO for budget, with Catherine Wilson (Legal) owning contract and Michael White (Head of Software Engineering) / IT owning integration and InfoSec. The buyer is now running the SOW and MSA contracting stage.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are clear from the structured POC and sandbox sessions: ease of use and low click-count requisitioning, catalog and basket experience with product images (Lauren Ross, 2 Jun 2026), GenAI keyword-free product search, automated three-way invoice matching, GenAI-assisted RFP creation and two-envelope sourcing (Matt, 18 May 2026), supplier onboarding and recommendations, and Workday integration as the single source of truth. Zycus won the POC as the 'best platform' (Next Step), showing these criteria were met. Merlin Intake AI and the agentic sourcing framework were a stated area of interest for Andrew and Elaine.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 42.

Their advocacy is proven by the 30 Jun 2026 decision to partner with Zycus. The remaining development is champion access to power: neither has yet pulled Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) or the CFO into a direct budget conversation.

cro_panel.header: Bright Horizons Family Solutions — Shortlisted · $251K · closes 31 Jul

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 42 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 46 (roughly flat): Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 34 is anchored on the stage win (42), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 46, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 / Moderate. Calls show genuine appetite for the GenAI search and agentic sourcing framework (Andrew's stated interest, POC access to autonomous agents), slightly under-rated versus the single-module AI scope. Merlin Intake is in scope as Phase 2 upside.

Forecast raised to Commit. Last verified buyer activity 2 Jul 2026.

critical_signals.text: Bright Horizons confirmed on 30 June it has chosen to partner with Zycus after the POC came back as the best platform.
Zycus was selected over the GEP and Coupa shortlist, so the only remaining threat is a timing-driven delay that lets the Proactis status quo linger.
The Order Form and revised UK-entity MSA went out on 2 July, but budget sign-off from VP Procurement Joanne Urso and the CFO is not yet in writing.
Bright Horizons asked for prep time before the SOW scoping workshop, so that session is not yet booked with under a month to the close date.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and rising into contracting: near-daily buyer emails through 1-2 Jul, selection confirmed, MSA and Order Form in motion. Not cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: 28 days to close (31 Jul, today 3 Jul). Created 27 May 2025, ~402 days old. Selection confirmed 30 Jun ('you have chosen to partner with Zycus'). Order Form + revised MSA (UK entity) sent 2 Jul; SOW scoping workshop being scheduled but not yet booked (BH flagged 1 Jul that Monday is a rush and they need prep). Legal (Catherine Wilson) and InfoSec/IT (Michael White) not yet engaged this cycle. A 2 to 3 week SOW + legal + InfoSec cycle starting this week lands ~22 to 26 Jul, preserving 31 Jul IF the workshop books this week and the order form is countersigned. Any slip in booking the workshop pushes signature into August. Pace required: lock SOW dates by 8 Jul, countersign Order Form/MSA by 14 Jul, legal + InfoSec in parallel by 24 Jul.
evidence: Selection confirmed: Claire Hudson to Elaine Rymill, 30 Jun 2026 email, 'Super excited that you have chosen to partner with Zycus... collating the pack to send across, including the SOW template and revised MSA'.
Contracting underway: Order Form plus updated MSA amended to the UK entity sent to Elaine Rymill on 2 Jul 2026 (SF task, Clari email).
Forecast raised Best Case to Commit by Anthony Gray on 1 Jul 2026 (field history), consistent with a confirmed selection.

Timing is the sole open risk: BH replied 1 Jul that 'we do need to do a little engagement and prep this end, Monday will be a rush for us', so the SOW scoping workshop is not yet booked with 28 days to close.

Economic buyer sign-off not yet evidenced in writing: Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and the CFO were referenced for a Boston executive validation meeting (internal note 16 Jun) but neither has appeared on a call.
north_star_verdict.headline: Bright Horizons confirmed selection of Zycus on 30 Jun after a successful POC (best platform), and both sides are now in SOW and MSA contracting. The deal is genuinely won-in-principle; the only real question is whether SOW scoping, legal redlines and InfoSec can complete by the 31 Jul close, which is tight.

flags: Convert the Boston exec discussion (Joanne Urso, VP Procurement, plus CFO) into written $251k budget sign-off before signature, the only power gap left on a selected deal.
Multi-thread now into Catherine Wilson (Legal) and Michael White (IT/InfoSec) so the SOW, MSA redlines and security review run in parallel, not in sequence.
Anchor SOW and signature to a mutual close plan tied to the Nov 2026 kickoff and Mar 2027 Proactis cutover so timing cannot cause drift into August.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a selected, contracting deal; the win-strategy levers are converting exec budget approval to writing, multi-threading into legal and IT for the July paper cycle, and holding the timeline to a mutual close plan.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the selected vendor as of 30 Jun 2026, having won the POC ('best platform') over the GEP and Coupa shortlist. GEP and Coupa are now declined (out-competed at selection). Proactis is the incumbent P2P provider being displaced (contract expiry Mar 2027). The single strongest CURRENT threat is Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal driven by timing: if SOW, legal and InfoSec slip past the Nov 2026 kickoff window, BH could defer or extend Proactis. CLM specialists (iCertis, SIREN Labs) and Checker were early-2025 peripheral mentions, now dormant.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal
competitors.quote: BH replied 1 Jul 'we do need to do a little engagement and prep this end, Monday will be a rush'; SOW workshop not yet booked with 28 days to close and finance constraints noted historically.
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor a mutual close plan to the Nov 2026 kickoff and Mar 2027 Proactis cutover; book the SOW workshop this week so timing cannot cause drift.

competitors.name: Proactis
competitors.quote: Proactis is the clunky incumbent P2P setup being replaced; lacks AI-driven intake and supplier risk automation, catalog management outdated.
competitors.how_we_win: Displace fully with the phased P2P-first rollout and the POC-proven three-way match and catalog UX; keep the Mar 2027 expiry as the forcing function.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: Shortlisted alongside Zycus and Coupa for the vendor selection; Zycus was chosen to partner on 30 Jun 2026.
competitors.how_we_win: Selection already won; keep the POC differentiation (ease of use, GenAI, integration breadth) front of mind through contracting.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Shortlisted alongside Zycus and GEP; not selected, Zycus chosen 30 Jun 2026.
competitors.how_we_win: Selection already won; ensure commercials stay within the validated envelope so price cannot reopen the field.

competitors.name: Checker (P2P alternative)
competitors.quote: Alternatives mentioned include systems like Proactis and Checker for P2P.
competitors.how_we_win: No longer in the frame post-selection; retain as history only.

competitors.name: iCertis (CLM)
competitors.quote: specialized CLM providers such as iCertis and SIREN Labs.
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus contracting/redlining covers CLM in-suite; no standalone CLM needed.

competitors.name: SIREN Labs (CLM)
competitors.quote: specialized CLM providers such as iCertis and SIREN Labs.
competitors.how_we_win: In-suite contract management removes the standalone CLM case.

Andrew and Elaine expressed interest in the agentic sourcing framework and were given access to autonomous sourcing agents in the POC, and GenAI features (RFP question generation, keyword-free search) landed well, but AI has not been positioned as the Phase 2 differentiator it could be.
